key_takeaways: Q4FY26 revenue grew ~22% YoY led by the cGMP (CDMO) vertical ramping on two new molecules; management reiterated a 20%+ revenue CAGR through FY28.
The Dahej cGMP-4 block was commissioned on schedule and is expected to contribute meaningfully from H2FY27 — the single biggest earnings lever in the model.
EBITDA margin held at ~23% despite the R32 refrigerant price correction; management guided to 25% in FY27 as high-value CDMO mix rises.
Long-term HPP (High Performance Products) contract renewals removed a key overhang; agrochem demand described as 'bottoming, not yet recovering'.
At ₹4,200 the stock trades at 44.8x FY27E / 34.4x FY28E EPS — a premium that already discounts flawless cGMP-4 execution; downside is real if the ramp slips.
Net debt inched up to ₹850cr on capex; management expects peak leverage this year and deleveraging from FY28 as new blocks turn cash-generative.
Verdict: Hold-watch — high-quality franchise, rich valuation. Add on a ramp-driven earnings surprise or a de-rating toward 30x FY28E.

products: High Performance Products (inorganic fluorides, refrigerant gases)

guidance.statement: Management reiterated a 20%+ revenue CAGR through FY28, front-loaded by cGMP-4 ramp.
Guided margins toward 25% in FY27 as high-value CDMO mix rises and R32 stabilises.
FY27 capex guided at ₹650-700cr, largely cGMP-4 completion and de-bottlenecking.
New Dahej block expected to contribute from H2FY27 with full utilisation over 18-24 months.

themes.evidence: Two new molecules moved to commercial supply; a third in validation. Order visibility described as 'strongest in company history'.
Spot R32 corrected ~15% YoY on Chinese oversupply; management called it 'a FY26 headwind we expect to lap by H2FY27'.
Described agro intermediates as 'bottoming, not yet recovering'; no restocking signal from global innovators yet.
Net debt at ₹850cr; management framed this as peak-capex-year leverage, deleveraging from FY28.

concall.tone_shift_vs_last_quarter: More confident on CDMO order visibility than Q3FY26 (moved from 'building pipeline' to 'commercial supply'), but noticeably more guarded on refrigerants and agrochem timing.

expansion_flags.driver: Two molecules to commercial + one in validation
Capex on cGMP-4 and Surat de-bottlenecking

thesis_triggers.evidence: Block mechanically complete; commercial supply from H2FY27 confirmed on call.
Two molecules moved to commercial supply this quarter.
Guided to 25% FY27 but Q4 held at ~23%; delivery not yet demonstrated.
Management explicitly said demand is bottoming, not recovering.

classification.justification: Revenue and margin broadly matched consensus; no positive or negative surprise.
20%+ CAGR and 25% FY27 margin targets restated, not raised.
Peak-capex year with leverage rising ahead of the cGMP-4 ramp.

risks.risk: cGMP-4 ramp slips or runs below target utilisation
Sustained R32 / refrigerant oversupply from China
CDMO revenue concentrated in a few molecules / innovators
Prolonged agrochem destocking delays HPP recovery
Rich valuation leaves little room for execution error

management_tone.anchor: 'Strongest order visibility in the company's history.'
'A FY26 headwind we expect to lap by the second half of FY27.'
'Bottoming, but we are not yet calling a recovery.'

thesis.point: cGMP (CDMO) is scaling into a structural growth engine — two molecules commercial, cGMP-4 commissioned, order visibility at an all-time high.
thesis.falsifier: cGMP revenue growth decelerates below ~20% for two consecutive quarters, or a flagship molecule is de-scoped by the innovator.

thesis.point: Mix shift to high-value CDMO lifts blended EBITDA margin toward 25%+, structurally re-rating earnings quality.
thesis.falsifier: Blended EBITDA margin stays sub-24% through FY27 despite CDMO mix rising.

thesis.point: India fluorochemicals + China+1 sourcing gives a durable, hard-to-replicate raw-material and IP moat.
thesis.falsifier: New domestic capacity from peers compresses CDMO pricing/returns.

anti_thesis.point: Valuation at ~45x FY27E EPS already discounts flawless cGMP-4 execution — the earnings surprise is priced in.
anti_thesis.falsifier: Stock de-rates toward ~30x FY28E while earnings keep compounding 20%+.

anti_thesis.point: HPP/refrigerant cyclicality (R32 oversupply, agrochem destocking) can offset CDMO gains and cap near-term margins.
anti_thesis.falsifier: R32 prices stabilise and agrochem restocking begins, lifting HPP utilisation.

anti_thesis.point: CDMO revenue is concentrated in a handful of molecules/innovators — single-contract risk is underappreciated.
anti_thesis.falsifier: Order book diversifies across 6+ commercial molecules with no single one above ~15% of CDMO revenue.

rows.driver: cGMP-4 ramp + CDMO molecule additions; HPP flattish until agrochem recovers.
Richer CDMO mix and better fluorspar sourcing.
Operating leverage on new blocks as utilisation climbs.
Mix shift to high-value CDMO; R32 headwind lapping.
EBITDA growth partly offset by higher interest and depreciation on capex.
Margin expansion outpaces the drag from capex-linked D&A and interest.

revenue_growth.basis: cGMP-4 contributes from H2FY27, full-year in FY28; two commercial molecules ramping, HPP flattish.

margin.basis: CDMO mix rises from ~34% to ~38% of revenue; R32 headwind fades from H2FY27.

assumptions.note: Model breaks if cGMP-4 utilisation lags the 18-24 month ramp, or if R32/agrochem weakness persists into FY28. Net-debt-funded capex means interest sensitivity is non-trivial.

valuation.sanity_check: At ₹4,200 (mkt cap ₹20,832cr, EV ₹21,682cr incl. ₹850cr net debt) the stock trades at 44.8x FY27E and 34.4x FY28E EPS, and ~28x FY27E EV/EBITDA. That is a premium to the ~35x 5-yr median PE and already prices in the guided ~23% revenue / ~27% PAT CAGR. The multiple is defensible only if cGMP-4 ramps on time and margins reach 25%+; any slip on either leaves meaningful downside. Multiples reconcile to inputs: 20832/3100 = 6.7x P/S FY27E; 21682/775 = 28.0x EV/EBITDA FY27E.

monitorables: cGMP-4 utilisation and quarterly CDMO revenue run-rate
Blended EBITDA margin trajectory toward the 25% FY27 guide
R32 / refrigerant spot pricing and inventory in the channel
Agrochem order inflow — first restocking signal
Net debt peak and the start of deleveraging
rerating_triggers: cGMP-4 ramping faster than the 18-24 month guide
A new marquee CDMO contract that diversifies molecule concentration
Margin surprise above 25% ahead of schedule
Agrochem demand inflecting, lifting HPP utilisation
next_steps.conviction_note: A high-quality fluorochemicals franchise with a genuine CDMO growth engine, but the ~45x FY27E multiple already discounts flawless execution. The risk-reward is balanced, not compelling. Prefer to add on either a ramp-driven earnings surprise that resets estimates higher, or a de-rating toward ~30x FY28E. Until then, watch the cGMP-4 utilisation curve and the margin bridge to 25%.
